content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 4.5.1-4.5.3 WARN — Push entitlement is used to schedule automated hourly promotional notifications (App/NotificationScheduler.swift:9-10, `scheduleHourlyDeals` repeating every 3600s), and metadata explicitly advertises "EVERY HOUR... unlimited notifications" (fastlane/metadata/en-US/description.txt:3), a spam-push pattern unrelated to user-initiated alerts.
Pierre: Apple's 4.5.3 explicitly bans using push for unsolicited promotional spam, and this code schedules recurring hourly marketing notifications with no evidence of explicit per-alert user opt-in or granular control. The metadata copy makes the intent unambiguous ("unlimited notifications, all day long"), which is a strong reviewer red flag. Fix by letting users configure alert frequency/categories and rephrase marketing copy to avoid promising unlimited/hourly pushes.
